pages.full_text:
school & out to look at the ducks. John was very pleased & relaxed, Charles, on his birthday, was doubtful about the whole thing & yelled when I put him on the saddle. But gradually he looked more confident & smiled. By the end, he was sitting up on his own (instead of leaning back against my arm) & stroking the horse's mane & [?serially] yelled when I got him off.

   We had tea in the van at the stables & set off for home to open Charles' presents.
pages.notes: Page is a continuation from the previous spread — the text begins mid-sentence with 'school & out to look at the ducks', describing a horse-riding birthday outing for Charles. The word I have read as '[?serially]' is unclear; it could be 'seriously' (i.e. 'seriously yelled'). No place names appear on this page. No year is stated; cannot be confirmed from this page alone.

pages.full_text:
__May 4th.__

   We set out from Birmingham on Saturday morning & called at the Wolverhampton camping shop to buy a cushion & deck chairs.

   We arrived at __Hamperley__, only to get thoroughly bogged down in the field. We had lunch bogged in & Simon worked for 2 hours to get us out, while the boys played with their hammocks in the woods.

   Simon took the 5 boys out for an hour's walk in the evening, just before rain came. It's cold & raining & very muddy.

__Sunday__ we again got bogged down; the man giving the [?funny] [?too] a [?race] list came & helped push us
pages.notes: The heading 'May 4th.' is underlined. 'Hamperley' is underlined; this may refer to Hamperley Farm or a campsite in that name — could alternatively read 'Hamperley' or '[?Hampersley]'. The final three lines of the page are harder to read: 'the man giving the [?funny] [?too] a [?race] list came & helped push us' — the phrase is partially cut off at the bottom of the image and the individual words are unclear; 'funny' could be 'sunny' or another word; '[?race] list' could be 'race list', 'grace list', or similar. No year is stated on this page; iso_guess is null as the year cannot be anchored from page content alone. Volume A covers 1989-2003; position at pdf_page 40 loosely suggests early-to-mid 1990s but this is not asserted.
